sources.notes: Statement and hard-version size bound n <= 50.
Official topic confirms the tridiagonal determinant recurrence and Fibonacci/golden-ratio predecessor search.
Independent writeup confirms the Euclidean-style tridiagonal construction.

annotation.summary: Construct a sparse {-1,0,1} matrix of size at most 50 with determinant x.
annotation.constraints: The hard size limit is too tight for the easy DAG gadget in worst cases, so a shorter recurrence-based construction is needed.
annotation.core_idea: Use a tridiagonal matrix. The determinants of leading principal minors satisfy A_{k+1}=A_k+A_{k-1} or A_k-A_{k-1} depending on signs. Reverse the process as a subtractive Euclidean sequence on a pair (x,y), choosing y through Fibonacci/golden-ratio guided search so the sequence reaches a small base pair within 50 rows.
annotation.complexity: O(50) recurrence generation plus bounded predecessor search
tricks: Choose a short predecessor sequence with Fibonacci/golden-ratio guidance.

solution_variants.name: tridiagonal-fibonacci-predecessor
solution_variants.summary: Find a suitable predecessor y, reverse the Euclidean subtraction sequence, and emit the tridiagonal matrix signs.

tags.evidence: The hard solution is built around the determinant recurrence of tridiagonal matrices.
tags.description: Use determinant recurrences or structural properties of tridiagonal matrices, whose nonzero entries lie on the main diagonal and adjacent diagonals.
tags.created_reason: The tridiagonal determinant recurrence is a reusable construction pattern not captured by generic determinant or matrix tags.

tags.evidence: The target value is controlled entirely through a determinant recurrence.
The recurrence is reversed by an Euclidean-style process on coprime adjacent determinant values.
The solution outputs an explicit bounded-size matrix from the recovered recurrence signs.
The constructed object is a sparse signed matrix; determinant/tridiagonal tags describe the main method.
